Undocumented instruction that loads the entire visible and hidden CPU state (all registers, including segment descriptor caches) from a fixed memory location. Opcode and memory layout differ between the 80286 (0Fh 05h) and 80386 (0Fh 07h); removed starting with the 80486. Historical/emulator interest only.

AVX: extract a doubleword from a selected lane of an XMM register into a GPR/memory.
AVX: extract a quadword from a selected lane of an XMM register into a GPR/memory.

AVX: insert a word from a GPR/memory into a selected lane (non-destructive 3-operand form).
AVX: insert a doubleword from a GPR/memory into a selected lane (non-destructive 3-operand form).
AVX: insert a quadword from a GPR/memory into a selected lane (non-destructive 3-operand form).
AVX: broadcast a single-precision float from memory (or, with AVX2, a register) to every lane of the destination.
AVX: broadcast a double-precision float from memory to every lane of a 256-bit destination.
AVX: broadcast a 128-bit block from memory to both lanes of a 256-bit destination.
AVX: insert a 128-bit block into one lane of a 256-bit destination, per an immediate selector.
AVX: extract a 128-bit lane from a 256-bit source, per an immediate selector.
AVX: permute 128-bit lanes between two 256-bit sources, per an immediate selector.
AVX: permute single-precision floats within each 128-bit lane, per an immediate or vector selector.
AVX: permute double-precision floats within each 128-bit lane, per an immediate or vector selector.
AVX: conditionally load/store packed single-precision floats per a per-lane mask register.
AVX: conditionally load/store packed double-precision floats per a per-lane mask register.
AVX: load the MXCSR SSE control/status register from memory.
AVX: store the MXCSR SSE control/status register to memory.
Bitwise AND of one operand with the one's complement of another (dest = ~src1 & src2), without a separate NOT step.
Extract a bit field from a register, with the start bit and length packed into a control operand.
Isolate the lowest set bit of an operand (all other bits cleared).
Set all bits up to and including the lowest set bit of an operand; clear the rest.
Reset (clear) the lowest set bit of an operand.
Zero all bits in an operand at or above a specified bit index.
Unsigned multiply, EDX:EAX/RDX:RAX implied as one source, writing both halves of the result to two explicit destinations, without touching flags.
Deposit low bits of an operand into positions selected by a mask, leaving other bits zero.
Extract bits from an operand at positions selected by a mask, packing them into the low bits of the destination.
Rotate right by an immediate count, without affecting flags and without a destructive read-modify-write on the source.
Shift arithmetic right, with the count in a register operand instead of CL, without affecting flags.
Shift logical left, with the count in a register operand instead of CL, without affecting flags.
Shift logical right, with the count in a register operand instead of CL, without affecting flags.
Add with carry using CF only (leaves OF untouched), for building carry chains independent of the ADOX chain.

Add with carry using OF only (leaves CF untouched), for building a second carry chain independent of the ADCX chain — the two can interleave in the same big-integer addition for instruction-level parallelism.
Perform one AES encryption round (ShiftRows, SubBytes, MixColumns, AddRoundKey) on a 128-bit state.
Perform the final AES encryption round (no MixColumns) on a 128-bit state.
Perform one AES decryption round (InvShiftRows, InvSubBytes, InvMixColumns, AddRoundKey) on a 128-bit state.
Perform the final AES decryption round (no InvMixColumns) on a 128-bit state.
Apply the Inverse MixColumns transformation to a round key, converting it for use in the equivalent-inverse-cipher decryption schedule.
Assist in generating an AES round key from the previous one, per a round-constant immediate.
AVX: perform one AES encryption round (ShiftRows, SubBytes, MixColumns, AddRoundKey) on a 128-bit state. (VEX-encoded, non-destructive form).
AVX: perform the final AES encryption round (no MixColumns) on a 128-bit state. (VEX-encoded, non-destructive form).
AVX: perform one AES decryption round (InvShiftRows, InvSubBytes, InvMixColumns, AddRoundKey) on a 128-bit state. (VEX-encoded, non-destructive form).
AVX: perform the final AES decryption round (no InvMixColumns) on a 128-bit state. (VEX-encoded, non-destructive form).
AVX: assist in generating an AES round key from the previous one, per a round-constant immediate. (VEX-encoded, non-destructive form).
AVX: apply the Inverse MixColumns transformation to a round key (VEX-encoded).
F16C: convert packed half-precision (16-bit) floats to single-precision.
F16C: convert packed single-precision floats to half-precision (16-bit), per an immediate rounding-mode selector.
Read a hardware-generated random number from the on-chip DRNG into a register; sets CF to indicate success.
Read a hardware-generated random seed (higher entropy quality than RDRAND, intended for seeding software PRNGs) into a register; sets CF to indicate success.
Read the timestamp counter and the IA32_TSC_AUX MSR together (EDX:EAX:ECX), serializing so no earlier instruction can execute out of order past it.
Read the FS segment base address into a GPR, without a privileged MSR access.
Read the GS segment base address into a GPR, without a privileged MSR access.
Write the FS segment base address from a GPR, without a privileged MSR access.
Write the GS segment base address from a GPR, without a privileged MSR access.
Save enabled processor extended state (x87/SSE/AVX/etc., per XCR0) to a memory area.
Save enabled processor extended state to a memory area, using the 64-bit operand form of the save-area header.
Restore enabled processor extended state (x87/SSE/AVX/etc., per XCR0) from a memory area.
Restore enabled processor extended state from a memory area, using the 64-bit operand form of the save-area header.
Read an extended control register (XCR) into EDX:EAX; XCR0 reports which state components XSAVE currently manages.
Write an extended control register (XCR) from EDX:EAX; used to enable AVX/AVX-512 state for XSAVE to manage.
Clear the busy flag of a shadow-stack restore token in memory, ending supervisor shadow-stack-switch tracking for it.
Restore SSP (shadow stack pointer) from a restore token in memory, resuming use of a previously suspended shadow stack.
Increment SSP (shadow stack pointer) by a multiple of 4 bytes, skipping over shadow-stack entries without checking them.
Increment SSP (shadow stack pointer) by a multiple of 8 bytes, skipping over shadow-stack entries without checking them.
Read the current 32-bit SSP (shadow stack pointer) into a GPR.
Read the current 64-bit SSP (shadow stack pointer) into a GPR.
Save the previous shadow stack's supervisor token so it can later be restored, used when switching shadow stacks.
Set the busy flag of the current shadow stack's restore token, marking it in-use.
Write a 32-bit value directly to a shadow stack (supervisor shadow-stack-stack management only, not a general store).
Write a 64-bit value directly to a shadow stack (supervisor shadow-stack-stack management only, not a general store).
Write a 32-bit value to a user-mode shadow stack from supervisor mode.
Write a 64-bit value to a user-mode shadow stack from supervisor mode.

FMA3: alternately add (even lanes) and subtract (odd lanes) a product from an addend on packed double-precision floats (operand order 1-3-2: dest holds the first multiplicand and the addend/subtrahend).

FMA3: alternately add (even lanes) and subtract (odd lanes) a product from an addend on packed single-precision floats (operand order 1-3-2: dest holds the first multiplicand and the addend/subtrahend).

FMA3: alternately add (even lanes) and subtract (odd lanes) a product from an addend on packed double-precision floats (operand order 2-1-3: src1 holds the first multiplicand and the addend/subtrahend).

FMA3: alternately add (even lanes) and subtract (odd lanes) a product from an addend on packed single-precision floats (operand order 2-1-3: src1 holds the first multiplicand and the addend/subtrahend).
FMA3: alternately add (even lanes) and subtract (odd lanes) a product from an addend on packed double-precision floats (operand order 2-3-1: src2 holds the addend/subtrahend).
